afd.snippet: 000 FXUS65 KGJT 220451 AFDGJT Area Forecast Discussion National Weather Service Grand Junction CO 1051 PM MDT Tue Jul 21 2026 .KEY MESSAGES... - Showers and storms will continue this week each afternoon and evening. The greatest coverage will be on Wednesday. - Primary threats include heavy rainfall, lightning, and outflow winds up to 30-50 mph. Hail cannot be ruled out in stronger storms. - Storm

alerts.title: Mud Slide
alerts.description: Between Exit 97: Silt (2 miles east of the Silt area) and Exit 105: New Castle (near New Castle) from Mile Point 101.5 to Mile Point 105. Road closed expect delays due to a mud slide. See map for detour(s). Comment: Traffic detoured via US-6. Please plan for delays this morning!

alerts.title: Paving Operations-139809
alerts.description: Between US 6 and Exit 90: CO 13; Rifle (near Rifle) from Mile Point 86 to Mile Point 87. Left lane closed due to paving operations. Width limit in effect. Width limit 12'0". Starting Sunday at 12:00AM MDT until July 24, 2026 at about 11:59PM MDT. Full schedule below: • July 19, 12:00AM - July 24, 11:59PM Comment: 24/7 eastbound left lane closure. For more information email: RulisontoRifleResurfacing@gmail.com.

alerts.title: Paving Operations-139820
alerts.description: Between US 6 and Exit 87: US 6; Rifle (3 miles west of the Rifle area) from Mile Point 83 to Mile Point 86. Right lane closed due to paving operations. Width limit in effect. Width limit 12'0". Starting July 23, 2026 at 9:00AM MDT until July 23, 2026 at about 7:30PM MDT. Full schedule below: • July 23, 9:00AM - July 23, 7:30PM Comment: Speed reduced to 55 mph and up to 20-minute delays. Wide loads detour from I-70 onto US 6, eastbound us Exit 81 and westbound use Exit 87. For more information email: RulisontoRifleResurfacing@gmail.com.
